description:
Goal: make top-level definition syntax in compiler2 work the Elixir way: definition spellings expand as compiler macros and hand canonical quoted fragments back through `Fz.Compiler.define(..., __ENV__)`, while function-body expansion stays lazy behind demand.

Grounding: compiler2 now has a stable quoted-source transport, a fact-based macro pipeline, a compile-time-only macro backend, and a staged `FunctionSource -> ExpandedFunctionSource -> FunctionDefined` boundary. The remaining architectural split is that source-spelled top-level defs/modules/protocols/impls can still enter publication directly instead of through the same compiler callback path macro-produced definitions use.

Strategy: define one canonical compiler-definition surface, move top-level definition families onto compiler macros in the compiler prelude, keep raw function facts lazy, then delete the direct publication path and restate the invariants.

Signal: top-level scope-shaping definitions enter compiler2 through one compiler callback, `FunctionSource` remains raw and demand-expanded, nested discovery/publication follows the same fragment path, and the remaining `fz-rh2.16.4` surface work sits above a simpler seam.
title: Compiler2 definition macros: make top-level defs expand through one compiler callback
